segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: And part of that means talking about the emotional impact the performance had on us.
segments.zh: 其中一部分就是要谈谈这场演出给我们带来的情感冲击。我觉得这很重要。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I think that's important.

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。我们肯定要提到导演处理剧中重要场景的方式有多好——比如罗密欧爬上朱丽叶阳台的那一幕。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: And we should definitely mention how well the director handled important bits of the play – like when Romeo climbs onto Juliet's balcony.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: And the fight between Mercutio and Tybalt.
segments.zh: 还有茂丘西奥和提伯尔特之间的打斗。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 对。提一下剧场空间以及导演如何利用它也会很有趣，但我觉得800字里写不下。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: It would also be interesting to mention the theatre space and how the director used it but I don't think we'll have space in 800 words.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: No. OK.
segments.zh: 是的。好吧，听起来都挺直接的。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: That all sounds quite straightforward.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: So what about The Emporium Theatre's production of the play?
segments.zh: 那么，关于Emporium剧院制作的这部戏呢？

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I thought some things worked really well but there were some problems too.
segments.zh: 我觉得有些地方处理得很好，但也有一些问题。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。比如布景怎么样？

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: What about the set, for example?

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I think it was visually really stunning.
segments.zh: 我觉得视觉上真的很震撼。要我说，这可能是这部作品最令人印象深刻的地方了。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I'd say that was probably the most memorable thing about this production.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: You're right.
segments.zh: 你说得对。舞台设计确实很惊艳，但其实我在其他作品中也见过类似的设计。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: The set design was really amazing, but actually I have seen similar ideas used in other productions.

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: What about the lighting?
segments.zh: 那灯光呢？有些场景灯光太暗了，很难看清楚。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Some of the scenes were so dimly lit it was quite hard to see.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I didn't dislike it.
segments.zh: 我并不讨厌。它有助于改变那些安静场景的氛围。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: It helped to change the mood of the quieter scenes.

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: That's a good point.
segments.zh: 说得好。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: What did you think of the costumes?
segments.zh: 你觉得服装怎么样？

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I was a bit surprised by the contemporary dress, I must say.
segments.zh: 我得说，我对现代服装有点惊讶。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Yeah – I think it worked well, but I had assumed it would be more conventional.
segments.zh: 是啊——我觉得效果不错，但我原以为会更传统一些。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Me too.
segments.zh: 我也是。我喜欢开头的音乐，觉得乐手们很出色，但我觉得他们被浪费了，因为音乐在第二幕和第三幕没什么影响力。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I liked the music at the beginning and I thought the musicians were brilliant, but I thought they were wasted because the music didn't have much impact in Acts 2 and 3.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Yes – that was a shame.
segments.zh: 是啊，真可惜。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: One problem with this production was that the actors didn't deliver the lines that well.
segments.zh: 这部作品的一个问题是演员台词说得不好，他们说得太快了。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: They were speaking too fast.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: It was a problem I agree, but I thought it was because they weren't speaking loudly enough – especially at key points in the play.
segments.zh: 我同意这是个问题，但我认为是因为他们说得不够大声——尤其是在剧中的关键点。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I actually didn't have a problem with that.
segments.zh: 我其实没觉得那是个问题。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: It's been an interesting experience watching different versions of Romeo and Juliet, hasn't it?
segments.zh: 观看不同版本的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》是一次有趣的经历，不是吗？

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Definitely.
segments.zh: 当然。这让我意识到这部剧仍然很有现实意义。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: It's made me realise how relevant the play still is.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。我的意思是，自莎士比亚时代以来，很多都变了，但很多方面又没变。青少年和父母之间总是有分歧和紧张关系。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I mean a lot's changed since Shakespeare's time, but in many ways nothing's changed.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: There are always disagreements and tension between teenagers and their parents.

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Yes, that's something all young people can relate to – more than the violence and the extreme emotions in the play.
segments.zh: 是的，这是所有年轻人都能产生共鸣的——比剧中的暴力和极端情感更能引起共鸣。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: How did you find watching it in translation?
segments.zh: 你觉得看翻译版怎么样？

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Really interesting.
segments.zh: 非常有趣。我本以为会更难理解，但故事我基本都能跟上。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: I expected to find it more challenging, but I could follow the story pretty well.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I stopped worrying about not being able to understand all the words and focused on the actors' expressions.
segments.zh: 我不再担心听不懂所有单词，而是专注于演员的表情。结局非常震撼。

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: The ending was pretty powerful.

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的，这反而让我觉得情感更强烈了。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: That somehow intensified the emotion for me.

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: Did you know Shakespeare's been translated into more languages than any other writer?
segments.zh: 你知道吗？莎士比亚的作品被翻译成的语言比任何其他作家都多。

segments.speaker: GEMMA
segments.en: What's the reason for his international appeal, do you think?
segments.zh: 你觉得他为什么能吸引全球观众？

segments.speaker: ED
segments.en: I was reading that it's because his plays are about basic themes that people everywhere are familiar with.
segments.zh: 我读到过，是因为他的戏剧主题基本，世界各地的人都熟悉。

items.prompt: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their reviews of Romeo and Juliet?

pairs.note: 题干中'emotional impact'与原文完全一致，是定位关键。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'reviews'和'include'在原文中直接出现，以及'emotional impact'完全匹配，可以定位到答案D。

items.prompt: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their reviews of Romeo and Juliet?

pairs.note: 题干'评论'对应原文'谈论'，是概括性替换。
完全相同的短语，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 学生同意需要包含情感影响，原文中'谈论情感影响'直接对应选项E。

pairs.note: 题干问的是布景，原文用'视觉上令人震撼'来替换描述布景的特点。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词'the set'在原文中没有直接出现，但通过描述布景的视觉效果'visually really stunning'来定位，这是对布景的间接描述。

pairs.note: 原文用'It'指代前文提到的'lighting'，是代词替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问'lighting'，原文中'It'指代前文讨论的灯光，因此定位到该句。

pairs.note: costume design（服装设计）在原文中被替换为contemporary dress（当代服装），属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的costume design对应原文中的contemporary dress，通过同义替换定位到答案。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干 'the music' 在原文中直接出现，可快速定位到该句。

pairs.p: actors didn't deliver the lines that well
pairs.note: 题干名词短语对应原文中否定句，说明演员台词表现不好。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问演员的台词表现，原文直接说演员台词表现不好，因此选F（否定评价）。

items.prompt: The students think the story of Romeo and Juliet is still relevant for young people today because

pairs.q: still relevant for young people today
pairs.note: 题干说'仍然相关',原文说'什么都没变',意思相同。
题干问原因,原文用'总是有'引出原因。

pairs.q: the story of Romeo and Juliet
pairs.note: 题干说'罗密欧与朱丽叶的故事',原文用'莎士比亚时代'指代同一故事。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句说'什么都没变,青少年和父母之间总有分歧和紧张',对应题干问'为什么故事仍然相关',所以选B。

items.prompt: The students found watching Romeo and Juliet in another language

pairs.q: watching Romeo and Juliet in another language
pairs.p: not being able to understand all the words
pairs.note: 题干说'另一种语言'，原文说'听不懂所有词'，这是对同一情境的不同描述。
'found'表示发现/感受，原文'stopped worrying'体现了态度的转变，属于同义表达。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问学生对看另一种语言的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》的感受，定位句提到'不再担心听不懂'，说明学生适应了，对应选项C（表示积极或适应）。

items.prompt: Why do the students think Shakespeare's plays have such international appeal?
